name: azure-observability
description: Azure Observability Services including Azure Monitor, Application Insights, Log Analytics, Alerts, and Workbooks. Provides metrics, APM, distributed tracing, KQL queries, and interactive reports.
capabilities: Query Azure Monitor metrics and logs using CLI commands
Write and execute KQL (Kusto Query Language) queries against Log Analytics
List and manage Application Insights resources
Configure OpenTelemetry for Python, Java, and TypeScript
Set up alerts and monitoring dashboards in Azure
Reference SDK quick guides for programmatic access
author: microsoft
securitySummary: This is an official Microsoft documentation skill containing only markdown reference files. All 57 static findings are false positives: backtick commands are CLI examples in documentation, URLs point to Microsoft Learn documentation, and environment variable patterns are standard configuration. No malicious behavior detected.
valueStatement: This skill provides Claude with knowledge of Azure monitoring services, Application Insights, Log Analytics, KQL queries, and SDK references for implementing full-stack observability in Azure applications.
userTitle: Azure Observability with Monitor, App Insights, and KQL
limitations: Does not create or modify Azure resources directly
Requires Azure subscription and appropriate RBAC permissions
MCP tools require Azure MCP server to be enabled
Does not provide real-time alerting configuration code

useCases.title: Debug application errors with App Insights
useCases.description: Query Application Insights to find recent exceptions, failed requests, and performance issues in production applications.

useCases.title: Analyze infrastructure metrics
useCases.description: Use Azure Monitor and Log Analytics to query CPU, memory, and disk metrics across Azure resources.

useCases.title: Build custom monitoring dashboards
useCases.description: Create KQL queries for custom reports and integrate with Azure Workbooks for interactive visualizations.

promptTemplates.title: Query recent errors
promptTemplates.prompt: Show me how to query recent errors from Application Insights using KQL to find exceptions in the last hour.

promptTemplates.title: Check resource performance
promptTemplates.prompt: Write a KQL query to get average response time and request count from App Insights, grouped by operation name.

promptTemplates.title: Set up OpenTelemetry
promptTemplates.prompt: Show me how to configure Azure Monitor OpenTelemetry for a Python FastAPI application with custom span attributes.

promptTemplates.title: Monitor Azure infrastructure
promptTemplates.prompt: Give me CLI commands to list Log Analytics workspaces and query CPU metrics for virtual machines.

bestPractices: Use KQL queries with time filters (ago()) to limit result sets and improve performance
Configure OpenTelemetry at application startup before importing other instrumented libraries
Use AAD authentication instead of instrumentation keys for production workloads
antiPatterns: Avoid querying large time ranges without filters - this causes slow queries and high costs
Do not hardcode connection strings in source code - use environment variables
Avoid logging sensitive user data in Application Insights - this creates compliance issues

faq.question: What is the difference between Azure Monitor and Application Insights?
faq.answer: Azure Monitor is the core platform for collecting metrics and logs. Application Insights is an APM (Application Performance Monitoring) solution built on Azure Monitor, focused on application-level observability with distributed tracing.

faq.question: Do I need an Azure subscription to use this skill?
faq.answer: Yes, this skill provides reference documentation for Azure services. Actual queries require an Azure subscription with Log Analytics workspaces or Application Insights resources.

faq.question: Can this skill create Application Insights resources?
faq.answer: No, this skill provides documentation and query examples. Use the Azure CLI (az monitor app-insights component create) or Azure portal to create resources.

faq.question: What is KQL?
faq.answer: KQL (Kusto Query Language) is the query language for Azure Log Analytics and Application Insights. It is similar to SQL but optimized for log analytics with time-series data.

faq.question: How do I enable the Azure MCP server?
faq.answer: Run /azure:setup or enable the Azure MCP via /mcp command. The MCP provides direct access to monitor_metrics_query, monitor_logs_query, and kusto_query commands.

faq.question: Is OpenTelemetry supported for all Azure services?
faq.answer: OpenTelemetry is supported for many Azure services via the Azure Monitor OpenTelemetry Distro. Check the SDK documentation for the latest supported languages and frameworks.
